name: Rebecca Kilgore
abstract: Rebecca Kilgore (September 24, 1949 – January 7, 2026) was an American jazz vocalist based in Portland, Oregon. She has been called "one of the best interpreters of the Great American Songbook".

interviews.title: Rebecca Kilgore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

concerts.label: Standard Time with Michael Feinstein: Bob and Bing: Two for the Road
Carnegie Hall Notables: The Ninth Annual Notable Occasion: Rossano Sportiello and His American Jazz Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Rebecca works all the time in Portland, freelance and with Dave Frishberg, and she’s on this jazz party circuit. So that’s enough right there... I mean how can you not like Rebecca’s singing or Bobby’s clarinet?
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: my favorite gig was with Becky Kilgore... we had a job together in a hotel two nights a week for five years. And just a duo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: One of his latest recordings is with Arbors artist Rebecca Kilgore, entitled “I Saw Stars.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, that’s Becky Kilgore. Yeah, I recognized me, in fact, is that on the record?
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: He has performed with Leon Redbone, Keith Ingham, and Rebecca Kilgore
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997
